mems.content: Lifecycle compact trace: User chose to start new task (option 2). Archived previous remediation trajectory for potential future reference.

Lifecycle compact trace: Completed marketing canvas and 19-slide presentation for Sữa Ông Thọ Tết 2026 campaign. Files saved: marketing-canvas-sua-ong-tho-tet-2026-02-19.md and presentation-sua-ong-tho-tet-2026-02-19.md

Lifecycle compact trace: Created market-research-framework skill (Technique type) covering Consumer Research, Market Sizing, and Trend Analysis. Bilingual (EN/VI), includes data validation tiers, TAM/SAM/SOM formulas, sample size guidelines, and evidence discipline rules.

Root Cause: HiveFiver journey contracts show boundary and schema normalization gaps under stress. Symptoms: todo.updated camelCase fields are dropped, action-level session updates create unbounded graph tasks, and event-handler hook writes tasks manifest directly (CQRS leak). Verification: code evidence in src/hooks/event-handler.ts, src/tools/hivemind-session.ts, src/lib/graph-io.ts plus runtime scratch simulation via npx tsx showing field-loss behavior. Files Changed: none (read-only audit).
Lifecycle compact trace: Completed commit 826e09ea context mapping review for hierarchy normalization changes

Lifecycle compact trace: Closing Cycle8 session to start fresh grand audit. Previous session completed Cycles 1-7 (session-ID parity, stale-task reconciliation, session-token continuity, HF-HARDEN 07-12). Cycle8 research was in progress for classifying remaining pending graph/state tasks.

Architecture Layer Audit KEY FINDINGS: 1) 4 import direction violations (lib→hooks: session-governance, session_coherence, compaction-engine, auto-commit all import from hooks/sdk-context). Root cause: SDK client access pattern. 2) 9 files exceed 300 LOC limit - graph-io.ts at 1350 LOC is worst (4.5x). 3) CQRS mutation queue pattern works correctly - hooks use queueStateMutation(). 4) All 6 tools are boundary-compliant. 5) Silent error swallowing in graph-io.ts and session-engine.ts. P0 fix: Create src/sdk/ layer, decompose graph-io.ts.

Schema/Data Model Audit KEY FINDINGS: 1) CRITICAL: BrainState, HierarchyState, HiveMindConfig, TaskItem have NO Zod schemas - TypeScript interfaces only, unsafe deserialization via JSON.parse() as T. 2) Dual hierarchy mismatch: 3-level cognitive (trajectory/tactic/action) in hierarchy.ts vs 6-level enterprise (project/milestone/phase/plan/task/verification) in graph-nodes.ts - no formal mapping between them. 3) FK validation comprehensive in graph-io.ts (22 UUID fields enforced). 4) PhaseNode validated on migrate only, not on read. 5) Dual persistence: hierarchy.json vs trajectory.json for trajectory data.

Orchestration/Integration Audit KEY FINDINGS: 1) 30 commands with significant alias overlap (init↔start, audit↔doctor, spec↔specforge). 2) Agent dual definition risk: opencode.json agent block AND .opencode/agents/*.md both define agents. 3) MCP partial coverage: only DeepWiki+Stitch enabled, Context7/Tavily/Exa disabled. 4) Skill overlap: delegation-intelligence ↔ sequential-orchestration ↔ context-first-gatekeeping cover similar ground. 5) Plugin bootstrap sequence is clean with proper SDK init order. 6) CQRS compliance confirmed at integration level. 7) Workflow overlap: hivefiver-enterprise vs hivefiver-enterprise-architect.

Runtime State/Pipeline Audit KEY FINDINGS: 1) DUAL TASK SYSTEM: state/tasks.json (HiveFiver fields) vs graph/tasks.json (FK-linked) - no sync mechanism. 2) DUAL MEMORY PATH: memory/mems.json (legacy) vs graph/mems.json (FK-linked) - cognitive-packer falls back. 3) Orphan quarantine bloat: 60+ entries with duplicates in orphans.json (race condition). 4) Inconsistent locking: only brain.json+graph/tasks+graph/mems have locks; hierarchy, anchors, memory/mems have NONE. 5) FK validation on READ only, not WRITE - allows orphan creation. 6) Manifest chain incomplete: codemap/codewiki are empty placeholders. 7) Session-ID mismatch between brain.json and trajectory.json. 8) graph/phases.json is orphaned file.

CROSS-DOMAIN SYNTHESIS: 17 issues found across 4 audit domains. 3 CRITICAL: (CF-01) Dual state systems — state/tasks.json vs graph/tasks.json, memory/mems.json vs graph/mems.json, hierarchy.json vs trajectory.json — NO sync mechanism between pairs. (CF-02) BrainState/HierarchyState/Config/TaskItem have NO Zod schemas — unsafe JSON.parse() as T in persistence.ts:159. (CF-03) 4 lib→hooks import violations due to SDK client access trapped in hooks/sdk-context.ts. USER JOURNEY RESULTS: Greenfield=PASS, Brownfield-resume=PARTIAL(divergence), Wrong-agent=FAIL(advisory-only), Corrupt-resume=FAIL(no validation), Novice=PASS, Expert=PARTIAL(workflow ambiguity), Parallel-stress=PARTIAL(unlocked files).

CONSOLIDATION ROADMAP (from SOT audit): Phase 1 (Week 1) Foundation Safety: Create BrainStateSchema+HierarchyStateSchema+ConfigSchema+TaskItemSchema in Zod, extend proper-lockfile to all state files, add FK validation on write, fix orphan quarantine idempotency. Phase 2 (Week 2) Architecture Repair: Create src/sdk/ layer, extract hooks types to schemas, decompose graph-io.ts (1350→3 files), decompose detection.ts (882→2 files). Phase 3 (Week 3-4) State Unification: Design canonical lifecycle contract, unify task/memory/trajectory dual systems. Phase 4 (Week 5) Surface Consolidation: Reduce 30→18 commands, merge skill overlaps, resolve agent dual definition. Phase 5 (Week 6) Hardening: Concurrency tests, schema migration tests, user journey integration tests, hard tool-gate enforcement.
